meaningEn: You damn fool! — a feisty Edo insult
meaningJa: ばかやろう（江戸っ子の啖呵）
exampleJa: べらぼうめ、なめんじゃねえ！
exampleEn: You damn fool — don't mess with me!
socialConsequenceEn: Yell this today and you instantly become a samurai-drama grandpa. Glorious, but nobody talks like this anymore.

tag.sourceNote: Edo-period rough speech; survives in jidaigeki

meaningEn: What're you on about?! — a blustering retort
meaningJa: 何を言ってやがる（の江戸訛り）
exampleJa: てやんでい、こちとら江戸っ子でい！
exampleEn: What're you on about?! I'm a true Edo native!
socialConsequenceEn: Peak Edo bravado. Say it and you sound like you stepped out of a 1960s samurai film.

tag.sourceNote: 「何を言ってるんだ」の江戸訛り

meaningEn: Get lost! (lit. 'come back the day before yesterday')
meaningJa: 二度と来るな（追い返す決まり文句）
exampleJa: そんな話、おととい来やがれ！
exampleEn: A deal like that? Get outta here!
socialConsequenceEn: A theatrical 'buzz off'. Hilarious and harmless now — pure old-Tokyo flavor.

tag.sourceNote: 来られない日を指す江戸の追い返し表現

meaningJa: 洗練されていない、無粋
exampleJa: それを聞くのは野暮ってもんだ。
exampleRomaji: sore wo kiku no wa yabo tte mon da.
socialConsequenceEn: Still perfectly usable today — an Edo word that never went out of style.

tag.sourceNote: 江戸の美意識(粋の対義)。現代も使う

meaningEn: Chic; stylish in an understated, cool way
meaningJa: 洗練されて格好いい、あか抜けた様子
exampleJa: あの人の着こなしは粋だね。
exampleRomaji: ano hito no kikonashi wa iki da ne.
exampleEn: The way they dress is so chic.
socialConsequenceEn: A compliment that still lands. Calling someone 粋 makes you sound cultured.

tag.sourceNote: 江戸の美意識の中核。現代も使う

meaningJa: 間抜け、とんちんかんな人をののしる語
exampleJa: このすっとこどっこいめ！
socialConsequenceEn: So old-fashioned it loops back to funny. Drop it as a joke and people will grin.

tag.sourceNote: 江戸の悪態。落語等で残る

meaningJa: 粋で勇み肌な様子
exampleJa: いなせな兄さんやね。
socialConsequenceEn: A vintage compliment. Sounds like you stepped out of a festival.

tag.sourceNote: 江戸の美意識(粋・勇み肌)

meaningJa: 生粋で歯切れがよい様子
exampleJa: ちゃきちゃきの江戸っ子だ。
socialConsequenceEn: Still used, a little old-timey. Charming.

tag.sourceNote: 「ちゃきちゃきの江戸っ子」慣用句

meaningEn: More than good enough; can't complain
meaningJa: 大いに有り難い・十分なこと
exampleJa: それなら御の字だ。
socialConsequenceEn: Still understood, sounds mature. Safe.

meaningJa: 自分(たち)を指す伝法な言い方
exampleJa: こちとら江戸っ子でい！
socialConsequenceEn: Tough-guy Edo speech. Pure jidaigeki energy.

meaningJa: 一文無し・空っぽ
exampleJa: 給料日前ですっからかん。
socialConsequenceEn: Edo-flavored but still used. Safe and vivid.

meaningJa: 江戸の伝法な話し方
exampleJa: べらんめえ口調で啖呵を切る。
exampleRomaji: beranmee chou de tanka wo kiru.
exampleEn: He rattles off in beranmee style.
socialConsequenceEn: The classic Edo tough-talk. Theatrical now.

meaningJa: 生粋の江戸生まれの人
exampleJa: あたしゃ三代続いた江戸っ子でい
exampleRomaji: atasha sandai tsuzuita edokko de i
exampleEn: I'm a third-generation Edoite, I am.
socialConsequenceEn: Worn with pride: quick-tempered, generous, can't save a yen.

term: 宵越しの銭は持たねえ
reading: よいごしのぜにはもたねえ
meaningEn: I never keep money overnight (Edo bravado)
meaningJa: その日に稼いだ金はその日に使う心意気
exampleJa: 宵越しの銭は持たねえのが粋ってもんよ
exampleRomaji: yoigoshi no zeni wa motanee no ga iki tte mon yo
exampleEn: Spending it all today — that's the stylish way.
socialConsequenceEn: The famous Edoite flex: too cool to hoard cash. Gloriously broke.

meaningJa: ちょっと、の下町的な言い方
exampleJa: ちょいと、そこのお人
socialConsequenceEn: Light, rhythmic shitamachi speech. Instantly old-Tokyo.

meaningEn: The missus; the lady of the house/shop
meaningJa: 店や家の女主人・奥さん
exampleJa: おかみさん、勘定たのまあ
socialConsequenceEn: Warm shitamachi address for a shop's matriarch.

meaningJa: 主人・お得意様への呼びかけ
exampleJa: 旦那、お安くしときやす
exampleEn: Sir, I'll give you a good price.
socialConsequenceEn: Merchant flattery for a valued patron. Still half-alive today.

meaningJa: ごめんください、の俠客的挨拶
exampleJa: ごめんなすって、お控えなすって
exampleEn: Pardon me — allow me to introduce myself.
socialConsequenceEn: Pure jidaigeki gambler greeting. Tarantino-cool, old-school.

meaningJa: 誠に申し訳ない、の下町言葉
exampleJa: 遅れちまってあいすまねえ
socialConsequenceEn: Gruff but heartfelt. The Edoite apology.

meaningJa: ありがとう、のぶっきらぼうな形
exampleJa: 世話んなったな、ありがとよ
socialConsequenceEn: Tough-guy gratitude. Warm under the gruffness.

meaningJa: お金の古い言い方
exampleJa: 銭がねえなら帰んな
exampleRomaji: zeni ga nee nara kaen na
socialConsequenceEn: Grittier than 'okane'. Smells of old coppers and market stalls.

meaningJa: お金が全くないこと
exampleJa: 月末はいつも一文無しよ
exampleEn: I'm always dead broke by month's end.
socialConsequenceEn: Lit. 'not one mon coin'. Vivid Edo-era broke.

meaningEn: A piece of cake; dead easy
meaningJa: とても簡単なこと
exampleJa: そんなの朝飯前よ
socialConsequenceEn: Lit. 'before breakfast'. Still used; warmly old-school.

meaningEn: No big deal; nothing to it
meaningJa: なんでもないこと、容易いこと
exampleJa: この坂なんざ屁の河童だ
socialConsequenceEn: Earthy Edo idiom (lit. 'a kappa's fart'). Charmingly crude.

meaningJa: そそっかしい人
exampleJa: また忘れたのか、おっちょこちょいだな
exampleRomaji: mata wasureta no ka, occhokochoi da na
socialConsequenceEn: Affectionate Edo-flavored jab. Still widely used.

meaningJa: 間抜け、おたんこなす
exampleJa: このとんちきめ
socialConsequenceEn: Old comic Edo insult. Sounds funnier than it stings.

meaningJa: 間抜け・のろまをののしる語
exampleJa: なにぼやっとしてんだ、おたんこなす
exampleEn: Why are you spacing out, you dummy?
socialConsequenceEn: Theatrical Edo scolding. More comedy than malice.

meaningEn: A night owl; one who stays up late
meaningJa: 夜更かしする人
exampleJa: おめえは宵っ張りだなあ
socialConsequenceEn: Cozy old word for a late-nighter. Reads vintage.

meaningJa: 太陽の敬称
exampleJa: おてんとさまに顔向けできねえ
exampleEn: I can't face the sun (I'm ashamed).
socialConsequenceEn: Folksy reverence for the sun. Carries old moral weight.

meaningJa: 江戸風の、江戸湾でとれた
exampleJa: やっぱ江戸前の寿司は違うね
exampleRomaji: yappa edomae no sushi wa chigau ne
socialConsequenceEn: Still a badge of authenticity, especially for sushi.

meaningJa: 武士が使う一人称（謙譲）
exampleJa: 拙者、剣の道を志す者でござる
exampleRomaji: sessha, ken no michi o kokorozasu mono de gozaru
exampleEn: I am one who walks the way of the sword.
socialConsequenceEn: Pure samurai-drama 'I'. Say it today and you're cosplaying a ronin.

meaningJa: 「ある・です」の武士的表現
exampleJa: 委細承知でござる
socialConsequenceEn: The signature samurai sentence-ender. Instant jidaigeki.

meaningJa: ご苦労であった、という上位者の労い
exampleJa: 遠路はるばる大儀であった
exampleEn: You've come a long way — well done.
socialConsequenceEn: A lord's stately 'good work'. Pure period-drama authority.

meaningEn: It's quite all right; you may (a lord's leave)
meaningJa: 差し支えない、構わぬ（上位者の許可）
exampleJa: 苦しゅうない、近う寄れ
socialConsequenceEn: A lord granting permission. Peak jidaigeki grandeur.

meaningJa: もっと近くに来なさい（上位者の命）
exampleJa: 遠慮するな、近う寄れ
socialConsequenceEn: Said by a lord on a dais. Total period-drama power move.

meaningJa: 罰すること、討ち果たすこと
exampleJa: 悪党め、成敗してくれる
exampleEn: Villain — I'll bring you to justice!
socialConsequenceEn: The hero's pre-fight line. Maximum samurai-drama energy.

meaningJa: 失礼する、許しを請う
exampleJa: 御免！通らせてもらうぞ
exampleEn: By your leave — I'm coming through!
socialConsequenceEn: The formal entrance/exit of a samurai. Crisp and proper.

meaningJa: 言う・名乗る、の謙譲語
exampleJa: 拙者、三十郎と申す
socialConsequenceEn: Humble self-introduction, samurai-style. Stately and old.

meaningJa: 武士は約束を違えない
exampleJa: 約束は守る。武士に二言はない
exampleRomaji: yakusoku wa mamoru. bushi ni nigon wa nai
exampleEn: I'll keep my word — a samurai never lies.
socialConsequenceEn: The honor-bound samurai oath. Heavy with old-school pride.

tag.sourceNote: bushi ni nigon wa nai proverb

meaningJa: 自分の家をへりくだって言う語
exampleJa: ぜひ拙宅へお越しを
exampleEn: Do come by my humble home.
socialConsequenceEn: Refined humble speech. Very period, very polite.

meaningJa: おまえ、を荒く言った語
exampleJa: 手前、よくも言いやがったな
exampleEn: You — how dare you say that!
socialConsequenceEn: Fighting words. Rough shitamachi/yakuza 'you'. Handle with care.

meaningJa: おい、こら、と呼びかける語
exampleJa: やい、待ちやがれ
socialConsequenceEn: A bristling Edo call-out. The sound of a brewing fight.

meaningJa: 野暮の極み、無粋な人
exampleJa: そこで聞くか、この野暮天が
exampleRomaji: soko de kiku ka, kono yaboten ga
exampleEn: You ask THAT now? Such a clod.
socialConsequenceEn: The anti-'iki': stronger than yabo. Edo aesthetics insult.

meaningJa: もちろんだ、という威勢のいい肯定
exampleJa: やるか？おうとも！
exampleEn: You in? You bet I am!
socialConsequenceEn: Hearty, chest-out Edo agreement. Full of bravado.

meaningJa: 当たり前だ、を崩した威勢のいい語
exampleJa: 勝てるかって？あたぼうよ
socialConsequenceEn: From 'atarimae da, beraboume'. Peak Edoite swagger.

meaningEn: Allow me to introduce myself (gambler's formal)
meaningJa: 俠客の自己紹介の口上
exampleJa: お控えなすって、手前生国は…
exampleEn: Allow me — my home province is…
socialConsequenceEn: The famous yakuza self-intro ritual. Pure jidaigeki theater.

reading: いよっま ってました
meaningJa: 歌舞伎などでの掛け声
exampleJa: いよっ、待ってました、中村屋！
exampleEn: Bravo! We've waited for this, Nakamuraya!
socialConsequenceEn: The kabuki audience cheer (kakegoe). Joyful old-Edo theater.
